Physical Address: Department of Chemistry and Polymer Research Institute, Pohang University of Science and Technology, Pohang, 790-784, Korea
Detector: refractive index detector; UV-visible absorption detector
Evidence: Critical Condition: The critical condition of liquid chromatography is the transition point of the separation mode between size exclusion and adsorption (or partition) when a porous column packing material is used for the separation of macromolecules. At the critical point of a particular polymer species, retention of that polymer becomes independent of the molecular weight.
Evidence: Critical Component: the critical condition of PEO block
Evidence: Column Name: a reversed-phase column (LUNA C18, 100-Å pore size, 250 × 4.6 mm)
Evidence: Mobile Phase Solvents: a mixture of acetonitrile (ACN, Aldrich HPLC grade) and water (deionized and filtered with a 0.45- _µ_ m filter)
Evidence: Mobile Phase Ratio: an ACN/water mixture at the ratio of 60/40 (v/ v)
Notes: Condition established for diblock copolymer analysis.

Evidence: Critical Condition: If the composition of the two solvents is 92/8 vol %, these conditions are obtained for a temperature of 29 C. In this case all 1,4-PI samples elute at the same retention volume irrespective of their molar masses.
